author: quartz
text:
§6 bl install — committed-subtree capability transfer

Symmetric capability-transfer logic for `bl install`: copy a committed
branch subtree from a --from ref to a --to ref (default terminus→landing),
git-free and dir-based so it unit-tests on tempfiles like change.rs; the git
ref-materialization and the atomic seal onto --to are the engine's job at
run-wiring.

One invariant dissolves two of the spec's named exclusions: the plugins
object copies only the relative-symlink wiring under config/plugins/ (dirs +
relative symlinks; bin/ subtree and every regular file skipped). The
committed <op>/<phase>/NN-<name> entries are exactly those relative symlinks,
so the LOCAL absolute bin/<name> never travels (recipient resolves locally)
and config/plugins/tracker/remote.toml — the file holding the trail next:
pointer — never travels (Pointer-exclusion), with no TOML parsing and no
reach into plugin territory (§0).

Object semantics (bl-0601): plugins/config REPLACE same paths (innermost
wins); tasks is the migration object — a UNION of both sides' tasks/<id>.md,
a same-id is a hard Conflict, never a silent clobber; non-destructive to
--from. Default bundle = [plugins, config] (tasks opt-in).

resolve_and_bind does the local binary resolution + protocol validation: the
edge supplies the candidate path (PATH or --bin name=path, kept env-free),
this refuses to bind unless the binary's <bin> protocol self-describe handles
every wired op and speaks the protocol version, then binds via the registry.

Exposes registry::{parse_entry, replace_symlink} as pub(crate) so install
reuses the NN- parse and idempotent symlink-replace (single source of truth).
100% coverage; full lib+integration suite green.
